groundings.quote: Metaethical moral relativist positions are typically contrasted with moral objectivism.
Other views—variously called moral non-cognitivism, expressivism, anti-realism, nihilism, etc.
Other views—variously called moral non-cognitivism, expressivism, anti-realism, nihilism, etc.
Metaethical moral relativist positions are typically contrasted with moral objectivism.
Other views—variously called moral non-cognitivism, expressivism, anti-realism, nihilism, etc.—contend that moral judgments lack truth-value
There were certainly occasional discussions of moral disagreement—for example in Michel de Montaigne’s Essays or in the dialogue David Hume attached to An Enquiry Concerning the Principles of Morals.

proposed_edges.quote: Moral relativism is an important topic in metaethics.
in the dialogue David Hume attached to An Enquiry Concerning the Principles of Morals
a related view, the error theory, claims that moral judgments are always false
